title: L'ingénieux hidalgo Don Quichotte de la Manche - Tome I
summaries: "L'ingénieux hidalgo Don Quichotte de la Manche - Tome I" by Miguel de Cervantes is a Spanish novel published in 1605. A minor nobleman named Alonso Quijano reads so many chivalric romances that he loses his sanity and decides to become a knight-errant. Renaming himself Don Quixote, he recruits a simple farm laborer, Sancho Panza, as his squire. Together they embark on adventures where Don Quixote mistakes windmills for giants and inns for castles, unable to see the world as it truly is. (This is an automatically generated summary.)
subjects: Spain -- Social life and customs -- 16th century -- Fiction
